message:
feat: automatic ffmpeg provisioning, bridge-native recovery fallback, and sync validation tests

Implements three major features across the audio pipeline, backend execution,
and sync subsystem, plus comprehensive test coverage for all of them.

Audio pipeline -- automatic ffmpeg provisioning (bd-194):
- src/audio.rs: Replace monolithic should_use_builtin_normalizer() with a
  multi-stage ffmpeg resolution pipeline: (1) check FRANKEN_WHISPER_FFMPEG_BIN
  env override, (2) check system PATH, (3) check state-dir provisioned binary,
  (4) auto-download static ffmpeg bundle (linux/x86_64 from johnvansickle.com),
  (5) fall back to built-in Rust normalizer
- New resolve_ffmpeg_program() / resolve_ffprobe_program() / resolve_tool_program()
  functions implement the cascading lookup with auto-provisioning via
  ensure_local_ffmpeg_bundle()
- Download uses curl (preferred) or wget with 180s timeout and 3 retries
- Extraction handles .tar.xz and .tar.gz archives, finds binaries via recursive
  directory search, copies to state-dir/tools/ffmpeg/bin/ and marks executable
- New env vars: FRANKEN_WHISPER_FFMPEG_BIN, FRANKEN_WHISPER_FFPROBE_BIN,
  FRANKEN_WHISPER_AUTO_PROVISION_FFMPEG (default: enabled),
  FRANKEN_WHISPER_FORCE_BUILTIN_NORMALIZE
- Add bool_env_enabled_from_raw() helper with unit tests for all true/false/
  default combinations
- probe_duration_seconds_with_timeout() and capture_microphone() now use
  resolved ffmpeg/ffprobe paths instead of hardcoded binary names

Backend execution -- bridge-native recovery (bd-6qt, bd-2w3):
- src/backend/mod.rs: In BridgeOnly execution mode, when bridge backend fails
  with a recoverable error (CommandMissing, BackendUnavailable, CommandFailed)
  and native engine is available, automatically retry with native engine
- New bridge_error_recoverable() classifies which FwError variants are safe
  to retry (excludes timeouts, cancellation, IO errors, invalid requests, etc.)
- New FRANKEN_WHISPER_BRIDGE_NATIVE_RECOVERY env var (default: enabled) with
  bridge_native_recovery_from_raw() parser supporting 0/false/no/off to disable
- Recovery sets execution_mode to "bridge_only_native_recovery" and preserves
  the original bridge error in native_fallback_error field
- available_for_mode() in BridgeOnly now also checks native availability when
  recovery is enabled
- 13 new unit tests covering bridge_error_recoverable for every FwError variant
  and bridge_native_recovery_from_raw for absent/false-like/non-false values

Sync subsystem -- validation and compression tests (bd-2l2, bd-1ao):
- src/sync.rs: Add comprehensive test suite for compress_jsonl/decompress_jsonl
  round-trip (empty, large, nonexistent input, corrupted gzip data)
- Add max_started_at() function tests covering empty DB, with data, with
  after_ts filter, and filter excluding all rows
- Add validate_sync() integration tests: valid after export, empty DB + empty
  export, detect run missing from JSONL, detect run missing from DB, and
  multiple matching runs

Integration tests:
- tests/cli_integration.rs: Add write_provisioned_ffmpeg_stub() and
  write_provisioned_ffprobe_stub() helpers creating shell script stubs
- New test: transcribe_file_input_falls_back_to_builtin_when_auto_provision_is_disabled
  verifies built-in normalizer is used when both PATH and auto-provision are off
- New test: transcribe_file_input_uses_state_dir_provisioned_ffmpeg_when_path_is_missing
  verifies state-dir provisioned ffmpeg stub is invoked via marker file
- New test: transcribe_bridge_only_mode_recovers_with_native_when_bridge_binary_missing
  verifies end-to-end bridge-native recovery including execution_mode and
  native_fallback_error in the output JSON

Documentation:
- README.md: Update prerequisites to note ffmpeg is optional with auto-provisioning;
  add 5 new env vars to configuration table; update normalization section and
  limitations to describe staged fallback behavior

Beads metadata:
- .beads/issues.jsonl: Updated issue bd-194 (ffmpeg auto-provisioning) status
  and added comment from agent OrangeDog
- .beads/.br_history/: 4 historical issue snapshot files

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1bk","title":"Foundation: Legacy Analysis, Project Infrastructure, and Porting Strategy","description":"EPIC: Foundation & Legacy Analysis

This epic covers the foundational work that ALL other work depends on. franken_whisper is a Rust-native ASR system that merges the best IDEAS (not code) from three legacy projects into something radically better:

- legacy_whispercpp (C/C++): Fast local inference, VAD (Silero), streaming, multi-platform HW accel, TinyDiarize, word-level timestamps, quantized models, 60+ CLI flags
- legacy_insanely_fast_whisper (Python): Flash Attention 2 (15x speedup), GPU batching, ergonomic CLI, pyannote diarization
- legacy_whisper_diarization (Python): Multi-stage pipeline (Demucs source separation → Faster-Whisper → CTC forced alignment → NeMo TitaNet speaker embeddings → multilingual punctuation restoration)

The merger pattern follows frankentui's proven methodology: SYNTHESIS not merger, CONCEPTS not copy-paste, DESIGN-DRIVEN not code-driven. The frankentui project demonstrated that planning before code (the first 48 hours were ALL planning) produces dramatically better results.

This project must deeply integrate with:
- asupersync: Cancel-correct async orchestration (not just Runtime::new — use Cx, regions, finalizers, budgets)
- frankensqlite: MVCC concurrent writes + RaptorQ durability (not just basic open/execute)
- frankentorch: GPU tensor operations + autograd (for acceleration beyond simple softmax)
- frankenjax: JIT-compiled transform graphs (for declarative computation paths)
- frankentui: Optional TUI for human operators

The project is BOTH a reusable Rust library AND a standalone CLI with robot mode (agent-first NDJSON) + optional TUI.

Key constraints: #![forbid(unsafe_code)], Rust 2024 edition, nightly toolchain, Cargo only, fsqlite only (no rusqlite).

## Acceptance Criteria
- All three legacy projects (whisper.cpp, insanely-fast-whisper, whisper-diarization) analyzed with strengths/weaknesses documented
- Cargo.toml validates with correct edition, features, dependencies
- All quality gates pass (fmt, check, clippy, test)
- FEATURE_PARITY.md tracks every legacy feature with migration status
- PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md reflects deep integration plan
- Porting strategy document is self-contained and actionable.","acceptance_criteria":"- All three legacy projects (whisper.cpp, insanely-fast-whisper, whisper-diarization) analyzed with strengths/weaknesses documented. - Cargo.toml validates with correct edition, features, dependencies. - All quality gates pass (fmt, check, clippy, test). - FEATURE_PARITY.md tracks every legacy feature with migration status. - PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md reflects deep integration plan. - Porting strategy document is self-contained and actionable.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:08:46.824625410Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T18:01:07.038840290Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T18:01:07.038735334Z","closed_by_session":"2026-02-22T13:01:06-05:00","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epic","foundation","phase-0"]}
 {"id":"bd-1bk.1","title":"Document legacy project analysis: what each project excels at","description":"Create a comprehensive analysis document (or update EXISTING_LEGACY_WHISPER_STRUCTURE.md) that catalogs:

FROM legacy_whispercpp (C/C++):
- Fast local inference via ggml (no ML framework dependency)
- Silero-VAD integration for speech pre-filtering
- Real-time streaming transcription (whisper-stream binary)
- Multi-platform HW acceleration: ARM NEON, AVX/AVX2, Metal, Core ML, CUDA/cuBLAS, Vulkan, OpenVINO
- TinyDiarize for lightweight speaker turn detection
- Word-level timestamps via -ml 1 flag
- Integer quantization (Q5_0 etc.) for model compression
- Multiple output formats: JSON, VTT, SRT, LRC, CSV, karaoke video
- HTTP server with multipart file uploads
- Confidence color-coding for terminal output
- Performance benchmarking tool (whisper-bench)

FROM legacy_insanely_fast_whisper (Python/Transformers):
- Flash Attention 2 support (15x speedup on long audio)
- GPU batching with configurable batch_size (default 24)
- BetterTransformer integration
- FP16 mixed precision
- Distil-whisper support for smaller model footprint
- Clean ergonomic CLI design (simple, opinionated)
- Direct URL input support (download and transcribe)
- Rich progress indicators
- pyannote.audio v3.1 diarization integration with HF token
- Speaker count control (num/min/max speakers)
- Word-level and chunk-level timestamp options

FROM legacy_whisper_diarization (Python/Faster-Whisper/NeMo):
- Multi-stage pipeline architecture:
  1. Demucs source separation (isolate vocals)
  2. Faster-Whisper ASR
  3. CTC forced alignment (ctc-forced-aligner for timestamp correction)
  4. MarbleNet VAD
  5. TitaNet speaker embedding extraction
  6. Speaker clustering and matching
  7. Multilingual punctuation restoration (12+ languages)
- Word-level speaker attribution (not just segment-level)
- Parallel processing mode (diarize_parallel.py) for >10GB VRAM
- SRT output with speaker labels
- Numeral suppression for alignment accuracy

For each capability, note:
- Whether it's already implemented in franken_whisper
- Whether it should be implemented natively in Rust vs bridged via subprocess
- Priority (essential vs nice-to-have)
- Dependencies on other beads

## Acceptance Criteria
- Document covers all three legacy projects with specific strengths per project
- Each project has: key features list, architectural pattern summary, performance characteristics, known limitations
- Cross-project comparison matrix exists
- Document is self-contained (no need to read legacy source).","acceptance_criteria":"- Document covers all three legacy projects with specific strengths per project. - Each project has: key features list, architectural pattern summary, performance characteristics, known limitations. - Cross-project comparison matrix exists. - Document is self-contained (no need to read legacy source).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:10:18.470490246Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T18:01:04.626866366Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T18:01:04.626800442Z","closed_by_session":"2026-02-22T13:01:04-05:00","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analysis","documentation","foundation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1bk.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1bk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:51Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-20g","title":"Robot Mode: Agent-First NDJSON Interface Enhancement","description":"EPIC: Robot Mode Enhancement

Robot mode is the agent-first interface — line-oriented NDJSON events streamed in real-time. This is how other programs (AI agents, automation scripts, CI/CD) interact with franken_whisper. It MUST be:
- Stable schema (versioned, backward-compatible)
- Deterministic where possible
- Explicit error codes (enumerated, documented)
- Line-oriented (jsonl/ndjson)
- Easy to pipe into other tools

Current implementation is functional but can be enhanced:
1. Schema versioning: Include schema_version in every event so consumers can adapt
2. Error code enumeration: Replace ad-hoc error strings with structured codes
3. Progress estimation: Include estimated_progress_pct in stage events
4. Streaming partial transcripts: Emit partial transcript text as segments are produced (not just at completion)
5. Backend availability query: `robot backends` command to list available backends with capabilities
6. Run comparison: `robot diff <run1> <run2>` to compare transcription results
7. Health check: `robot health` to verify system readiness
8. Input validation: `robot validate --input <file>` to check input before committing to a run

The event schema should be documented as a JSON Schema artifact that can be consumed by typed language generators.

Key files: src/robot.rs (112 lines), src/main.rs (robot command handling)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Robot mode outputs stable, versioned NDJSON schema
- All events have explicit error codes
- Schema versioning supports backward compatibility
- Event ordering is guaranteed monotonic
- All robot mode contract tests pass.","acceptance_criteria":"Robot mode outputs stable, versioned NDJSON schema. All events have explicit error codes. Schema versioning supports backward compatibility. Event ordering is guaranteed monotonic. All robot mode contract tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:08:47.305701227Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T20:46:02.725333019Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T20:46:02.725313763Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-ergonomics","epic","phase-2","robot-mode"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-20g","depends_on_id":"bd-1rj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-25T20:17:51Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-20g.2","title":"Enumerate explicit error codes for robot mode","description":"Replace ad-hoc error strings with structured, enumerated error codes.

Scheme: E1xxx (input), E2xxx (backend), E3xxx (storage), E4xxx (acceleration), E5xxx (sync), E6xxx (pipeline), E9xxx (internal).

Each code has: numeric code (stable, never reused), short name, human message, suggested recovery.

UNIT TESTS REQUIRED (Definition of Done):
- test_all_error_codes_unique: No duplicate numeric codes
- test_all_error_codes_have_name: Every code has a non-empty short name
- test_error_code_mapping: FwError variants map to correct error code ranges
- test_error_event_format: {"event": "run_error", "code": "E2001", "name": "backend.unavailable", ...}
- test_recovery_suggestions_present: Every error code has a non-empty recovery suggestion
- test_error_codes_stable: Error code list matches golden file (prevents accidental changes)

LOGGING:
- "Error mapped to code {code}/{name}: {message}" at ERROR per error occurrence

## Acceptance Criteria
- Error code enum covers all failure modes: backend_unavailable, input_not_found, ffmpeg_failed, normalization_failed, backend_error, storage_error, cancelled, timeout, unknown
- Each error code has human-readable message and machine-parseable code string
- Unit test: every FwError variant maps to a unique error code

 {"id":"bd-2xe","title":"TTY Audio: Low-Bandwidth Audio Relay and Streaming","description":"EPIC: TTY Audio Enhancement

The TTY audio system is a unique innovation — sending audio over terminal PTY/SSH channels where binary streaming is awkward. Current implementation: μ-law encoding + zlib compression + base64 + NDJSON frames.

This is genuinely novel. No legacy project has anything like it. Enhancement goals:
1. Adaptive bitrate: Detect available bandwidth and adjust chunk size/compression
2. Real-time streaming: Currently only file encode/decode; add live mic → NDJSON → remote decode
3. Error correction: Add sequence numbers with gap detection and optional FEC (forward error correction)
4. Pipeline integration: tty-audio input → direct transcription (encode → decode → transcribe in one pipeline)
5. Opus codec option: For higher quality at same bandwidth, offer Opus as alternative to μ-law
6. Bandwidth estimation: Report actual bandwidth usage in robot mode events
7. Two-way relay: Support bidirectional audio for potential voice interaction scenarios

Use case: SSH into a remote server, speak into local mic, have remote franken_whisper transcribe in real-time, stream results back. All over a plain TTY connection.

Key files: src/tty_audio.rs (204 lines)
Codec chain: Input → ffmpeg → 8kHz mono μ-law → chunk → zlib → base64 → NDJSON

## Acceptance Criteria
- TTY audio codec encodes/decodes audio via mu-law + zlib + base64 NDJSON frames
- Round-trip preserves audio quality within acceptable SNR
- Pipeline integration allows TTY-sourced audio to feed into transcription
- Adaptive bitrate adjusts to available bandwidth
- All TTY audio tests pass.","acceptance_criteria":"TTY audio codec encodes/decodes audio via mu-law + zlib + base64 NDJSON frames. Round-trip preserves audio quality within acceptable SNR. Command: franken_whisper tty-audio transcribe --chunk-ms 200

 {"id":"bd-efr","title":"Alien-Artifact Engineering: Adaptive Controllers with Formal Guarantees","description":"EPIC: Alien-Artifact Engineering

The AGENTS.md mandates that any adaptive/runtime decision must include:
- Explicit state space
- Explicit actions
- Loss matrix
- Posterior/confidence terms
- Calibration metric
- Deterministic fallback trigger
- Evidence ledger artifact

No adaptive controller should ship without conservative fallback.

Application areas in franken_whisper:
1. Adaptive Backend Router: Choose backend based on input characteristics (duration, estimated noise, speaker count, available hardware). State space = {input features}. Actions = {backend choice}. Loss matrix = {quality × latency tradeoffs}. Fallback = whisper_cpp (most reliable).

2. Confidence Calibration: Raw confidence scores from different backends are on different scales. Need a calibrated posterior that means "P(word is correct) = confidence_score". Calibration metric = Brier score or ECE (Expected Calibration Error).

3. VAD Threshold Tuning: Voice activity detection threshold affects precision/recall tradeoff. Too aggressive = miss speech. Too conservative = process silence. Adaptive threshold based on audio characteristics.

4. Two-Lane Execution: Run two backends in parallel, compare results, pick the one with higher calibrated confidence. This is the "alien" approach — use redundancy to improve quality.

5. Streaming Quality Controller: When streaming, decide when to emit a "stable" partial transcript vs waiting for more context. State = {accumulated audio, current hypothesis, confidence}. Action = {emit, wait}. Loss = {latency vs accuracy}.

Each controller must produce an evidence ledger artifact documenting the decision process for auditability and debugging.

STATE SPACE: Audio duration, noise estimate (SNR), diarization requested, available backends, GPU capability, historical performance.
ACTIONS: Select backend, model size, processing mode.

- test_adaptive_with_history: 100+ runs → adaptive selection based on posterior
- test_loss_matrix_values: All cells in loss matrix are finite, non-negative
- test_evidence_ledger_complete: Every adaptive decision produces ledger with ALL required fields
- test_evidence_ledger_stored: Ledger written to events table as stage=adaptive_routing
- test_brier_score_computation: Known predictions + outcomes → correct Brier score
- test_shadow_mode_event: Auto-selection emits backend.routing.shadow event with full state/action/loss

 {"id":"bd-qlt.8","title":"Implement CorrectionEvidenceLedger with per-window correction tracking","description":"PHASE 3 ADAPTIVE INTELLIGENCE -- Persistent evidence ledger that records every speculation decision for analysis, debugging, and adaptive improvement.

FILE: src/speculation.rs (extend)

WHY:
The existing RoutingEvidenceLedger (backend/mod.rs) tracks backend selection decisions. The CorrectionEvidenceLedger is its speculation analog: it records every window's fast/quality comparison, correction decision, and drift metrics. This serves three purposes:
1. Post-hoc analysis: "Why did the pipeline correct window 47?"
2. Adaptive control: The SpeculationWindowController reads this to tune window size
3. Debugging: Full replay of every decision for deterministic reproduction

RELATIONSHIP TO EXISTING EVIDENCE INFRASTRUCTURE:
- RoutingEvidenceLedger (backend/mod.rs:229-381) -- tracks backend selection, capacity 200
- PipelineCx::record_evidence() (orchestrator.rs) -- accepts Value entries
- EvidenceLedgerBuilder (accelerate.rs) -- builds acceleration evidence
- This new CorrectionEvidenceLedger follows the same pattern: structured entries, bounded capacity, JSON export

TYPES:

pub struct CorrectionEvidenceLedger {
    entries: VecDeque<CorrectionEvidenceEntry>,
    capacity: usize,
    total_recorded: u64,
}

pub struct CorrectionEvidenceEntry {
    pub entry_id: u64,
    pub window_id: u64,
    pub run_id: String,
    pub timestamp_rfc3339: String,
    
    // Fast model info
    pub fast_model_id: String,
    pub fast_latency_ms: u64,
    pub fast_confidence_mean: f64,
    pub fast_segment_count: usize,
    
    // Quality model info
    pub quality_model_id: String,
    pub quality_latency_ms: u64,
    pub quality_confidence_mean: f64,
    pub quality_segment_count: usize,
    
    // Drift metrics
    pub drift: CorrectionDrift,
    
    // Decision
    pub decision: String,          // "confirmed" or "corrected"
    pub tolerance_snapshot: CorrectionTolerance,
    
    // Controller state at decision time
    pub window_size_ms: u64,
    pub correction_rate_at_decision: f64,
    pub controller_action: String, // "shrink", "hold", "grow"
    pub controller_confidence: f64,
    pub fallback_active: bool,
    pub fallback_reason: Option<String>,
}

impl CorrectionEvidenceLedger {
    pub fn new(capacity: usize) -> Self
    pub fn record(&mut self, entry: CorrectionEvidenceEntry)
    pub fn total_recorded(&self) -> u64
    pub fn entries(&self) -> &VecDeque<CorrectionEvidenceEntry>
    pub fn to_evidence_json(&self) -> Value  // For PipelineCx::record_evidence()
    pub fn diagnostics(&self) -> Value       // Summary stats as JSON
    
    // Analysis methods
    pub fn correction_rate(&self) -> f64
    pub fn mean_fast_latency(&self) -> f64
    pub fn mean_quality_latency(&self) -> f64
    pub fn mean_wer(&self) -> f64
    pub fn latency_savings_pct(&self) -> f64  // How much faster user sees results vs quality-only
    
    // Window size trend
    pub fn window_size_trend(&self) -> Vec<(u64, u64)>  // (window_id, window_size_ms) pairs
}

INTEGRATION WITH PipelineCx:
At the end of a speculative run, the pipeline calls:
  pcx.record_evidence(&correction_ledger.to_evidence_json());
This makes the correction evidence part of the run's permanent evidence trail, alongside routing evidence and acceleration evidence.

INTEGRATION WITH STORAGE:
The evidence entries should be persisted in the RunReport.evidence array, which flows through to frankensqlite via the existing storage pipeline. No new storage schema needed.

CAPACITY & BOUNDS:
- Default capacity: 500 entries (more than routing ledger's 200, because windows are smaller units)
- Old entries are evicted FIFO when capacity is reached
- total_recorded tracks the true count even after eviction

 {"id":"bd-qlt.9","title":"Add TUI correction rendering with visual retraction markers","description":"PHASE 4 SURFACE INTEGRATION -- Makes corrections visible in the TUI.

FILE: src/tui.rs (extend existing, behind #[cfg(feature = "tui")])

WHY:
When the quality model corrects a fast model result, the TUI must show the user what happened. Without visual correction rendering, the user sees text appear, then silently change -- which is confusing and feels like a bug. With proper rendering, corrections are a feature: the user sees instant results with clear visual markers when corrections arrive.

CURRENT TUI STATE:
- LiveTranscriptionView (tui.rs:534-704): push_segment, scroll, auto_scroll
- SpeakerColorMap: ANSI color-coded speaker labels
- render_speaker_colored_line(): segment -> colored string
- SearchState, TranscriptFilter, TranscriptSearch: search/filter infrastructure

WHAT TO ADD:

1. CorrectionState tracking per-segment:
   pub(crate) enum SegmentCorrectionState {
       Original,       // Not part of any speculation (file mode, no --speculative)
       Speculative,    // Fast model result, pending quality confirmation
       Confirmed,      // Quality model agreed
       Retracted,      // Quality model disagreed, this segment is no longer valid
       Corrected,      // This segment is the quality model's replacement
   }

2. Extend LiveTranscriptionView:
   - Add correction_states: HashMap<usize, SegmentCorrectionState> (keyed by segment index)
   - Add push_speculative_segment(&mut self, segment, seq) -- marks as Speculative
   - Add retract_segment(&mut self, seq) -- finds segment by seq, marks Retracted
   - Add push_correction(&mut self, replaces_seq, corrected_segments) -- marks old as Retracted, new as Corrected
   - Add correction_count(&self) -> usize
   - Add speculation_stats_line(&self) -> String -- "Corrections: 3/47 windows (6.4%)"

3. Visual rendering rules:
   - Speculative segments: render with a subtle "~" prefix (e.g., "~ hello world")
   - Confirmed segments: render normally (remove "~" prefix)
   - Retracted segments: render with strikethrough ANSI codes (\x1b[9m...\x1b[0m) and dim color
   - Corrected segments: render with a brief highlight (bold for 2 ticks, then normal)
   - The correction transition should be visible but not jarring

4. Status bar extension:
   - Add speculation mode indicator: "[SPEC]" when speculative streaming is active
   - Show correction rate: "Corrections: 3/47 (6.4%)"
   - Show current window size: "Window: 3000ms"

5. Correction history panel (optional, toggled with 'c' key):
   - Shows last N corrections as a scrollable list
   - Each entry: "Window #47: 'hello wrold' -> 'hello world' (WER: 0.08)"
   - Useful for debugging and understanding pipeline behavior

DESIGN CHOICE -- WHY NOT JUST REPLACE TEXT SILENTLY:
- Users notice silent changes and find them unsettling
- Showing corrections builds trust ("the system is self-improving")
- The correction history is valuable for understanding model behavior
- Power users want to see correction rates to tune their model selection

BACKWARD COMPATIBILITY:
- When --speculative is not active, all segments are SegmentCorrectionState::Original
- No visual changes to non-speculative mode
- All existing TUI tests must continue to pass

 ## Limitations
 
 - **Backend binaries required.** franken_whisper orchestrates external ASR engines; it does not include inference runtimes. You need whisper.cpp, insanely-fast-whisper, or whisper-diarization installed.
-- **ffmpeg strongly recommended.** ffmpeg enables the broadest media/container support and is required for microphone capture. A built-in Rust fallback can normalize common file-based audio formats when ffmpeg is unavailable.
+- **ffmpeg support has staged fallback behavior.** franken_whisper first tries explicit/system binaries, then auto-provisions local ffmpeg/ffprobe (linux/x86_64), and finally uses a built-in Rust normalizer for common file-based formats when ffmpeg is still unavailable. Microphone capture still depends on ffmpeg availability.
 - **Path dependencies.** The project depends on sibling Cargo workspace members (`asupersync`, `frankensqlite`, etc.) via relative paths. It is not published to crates.io as a standalone crate.
 - **Native engines are pilots.** Native Rust engine implementations are deterministic conformance pilots. They can execute in-process when `FRANKEN_WHISPER_NATIVE_EXECUTION=1` and rollout stage is `primary|sole`; otherwise bridge adapters remain active.
 - **No bidirectional sync.** JSONL export/import is one-way. There is no merge or conflict resolution beyond the explicit `--conflict-policy` flag.
